business_forms.description: Sole proprietorship, simplest form, owner fully liable
General partnership, partners jointly liable
Private limited company, most common corporate form
Foundation, no shareholders, used for non-profit or holding structures

nl.social_security: Employer pays ~18% for social insurance (WW, WIA, ZW). Employee contributions via income tax (AOW, Anw, Wlz). Health insurance (Zvw) is mandatory private, ~€130/month plus employer contribution of 6.68%.

income_tax.name: Einkommensteuer (ESt)
income_tax.note: Plus Solidaritätszuschlag (Soli) of 5.5% on tax owed (exempted for most taxpayers since 2021)
income_tax.filing_deadline: September 2 (following year, with tax advisor: June following+1 year)

corporate_tax.name: Körperschaftsteuer (KSt)
corporate_tax.note: Plus Gewerbesteuer (trade tax) averaging ~14%, total effective ~30%

business_forms.description: Sole proprietorship, requires Gewerbeanmeldung
Civil law partnership, simple partnership form
Limited liability company, €25,000 minimum capital
Mini-GmbH, from €1 capital, must build reserves
Public limited company, €50,000 minimum capital

de.social_security: Split roughly 50/50 between employer and employee. Total ~40% of gross salary: health insurance ~14.6%, pension ~18.6%, unemployment ~2.6%, long-term care ~3.4%. Capped at income ceilings (Beitragsbemessungsgrenze).

business_registry.name: Registre du Commerce et des Sociétés (RCS) / Guichet unique INPI

income_tax.name: Impôt sur le revenu (IR)
income_tax.note: Rates apply per quotient familial (household shares). Additional 3% on income above €250k (4% above €500k).
income_tax.filing_deadline: May–June (exact date depends on département and filing method)

business_forms.description: Simplified sole trader regime with turnover caps and flat-rate charges
Sole proprietorship, personal assets now protected by default since 2022
Limited liability company, 2–100 partners
Flexible joint-stock company, most popular corporate form for startups
Public limited company, €37,000 minimum capital

fr.social_security: Managed by URSSAF. Employee social charges ~22% of gross, employer charges ~25–42% of gross. Covers health (Assurance Maladie), pension (retraite), family allowances (CAF), unemployment (Pôle emploi). Auto-entrepreneurs pay simplified flat-rate contributions (~12.3–21.2% of turnover).

immigration_portal.name: Office français de l'immigration et de l'intégration (OFII)

business_registry.name: Registro delle Imprese (via Camere di Commercio)

income_tax.name: Imposta sul reddito delle persone fisiche (IRPEF)
income_tax.note: Regional (IRPEF regionale, ~1.2–3.3%) and municipal surcharges (addizionale comunale, ~0–0.9%) apply on top.
income_tax.filing_deadline: October 31 (Modello Redditi online) or September 30 (Modello 730 via employer/CAF)

corporate_tax.name: Imposta sul reddito delle società (IRES)
corporate_tax.note: Plus IRAP (Imposta regionale sulle attività produttive) at 3.9% standard rate

business_forms.description: Sole proprietorship, owner personally liable
Limited liability company, most common corporate form
Simplified SRL, €1 minimum capital, reduced setup costs
Joint-stock company, €50,000 minimum capital
Limited partnership with general and limited partners

it.social_security: Managed by INPS. Employees: employer pays ~30%, employee ~9.2% of gross salary. Self-employed (Gestione Separata): ~26.07%. Artisans/traders: ~24% on income. Covers pension, sickness, maternity, unemployment (NASpI). Regime forfettario (flat-tax regime at 15% / 5% for new) available for small businesses under €85,000 turnover.

income_tax.name: Impuesto sobre la Renta de las Personas Físicas (IRPF)
income_tax.note: Combined state + regional rates. Autonomous communities may adjust their share.

corporate_tax.name: Impuesto sobre Sociedades (IS)
corporate_tax.reduced_rate: 15% for newly created companies (first two profitable years)

business_forms.description: Self-employed individual, must register with Seguridad Social
Limited liability company, €3,000 minimum capital, most common form
Public limited company, €60,000 minimum capital
Community of property, simple partnership for shared assets

es.social_security: Managed by Seguridad Social / TGSS. Employees: employer ~30%, employee ~6.4% of gross. Autónomos: income-based contribution system since 2023 (quotes range €230–€500+/month based on net income brackets). Covers healthcare, pension, sickness, parental leave, unemployment.
